course_description:
Independent research and thesis preparation under the guidance of a supervisor.

This course commences in Semester 2, and completes in Semester 2, Year 2 (three semesters duration for part-time enrolment).

Please note this Course Profile is relevant to the cohort who commenced the course in Semester 2, 2025.

SOSC6014 Honours Research Thesis refers to a program of part-time study in which the honours research thesis is completed over three consecutive semesters . Students are expected to complete the equivalent of 4 units of thesis work in each semester.

A thesis topic will be accepted only if Thesis Advisors are available in the general discipline area in which the student wants to study. This will be decided by the Honours Convenor in consultation with other staff members.

An honours degree is an intensive program of study following a three year Bachelor degree. For your honours thesis, you must address and meet the criteria and standards of assessment for the thesis set out in the marking guide available in the 'Assessment' folder on Blackboard. In addition to the written thesis, students are required to participate in a pass/fail oral defence hurdle about the thesis to assess whether they have attained the required learning outcomes of the thesis. The following are general guidelines:

incompatible: You can't enrol in this course if you've already completed the following:
restrictions: Restricted to students enrolled in the BA(Hons), BSocSc(Hons), BCCJ(Hons) or BSc(Hons)/BAdvSc(Hons) with Archaeological Science Field of Study.

course_contacts.name: Dr Jonah Rimer
course_contacts.notes: Dr Jonah Rimer is the School of Social Science Honours Convenor for 2026.

course_contacts.name: Student Enquiries School of Social Science
course_contacts.notes: Level 3, Michie Building (09), St Lucia campus, The University of Queensland. Monday-Friday, 9:00am-12:00pm, 1:00pm-4:00pm.

course_aims:
The main aims of the Honours year are:

• ﾠﾠ to enable students to pursue further study in a specialised area in more depth than is possible in a 3-year Bachelors degree program;
• ﾠﾠ to enhance students’ capacity for independent research and to acquire research skills appropriate to their areas of specialisation; and

•ﾠﾠﾠ to train to a standard sufficient for enrolment in postgraduate research degrees and for a specialist career in the field of research.

The Honours thesis constitutes a major component of the Honours program.ﾠ Other coursework complements the specific aims of the student's thesis research.

learning_outcomes.description: Apply a range of skills and analyse a body of knowledge, and thus to demonstrate the potential to succeed at more advanced levels
Contribute to the chosen field of study through the development of new ideas or critical review of existing perspectives

Demonstrate an ability to find and assess sources of information, and particularly to estimate the relevance of arguments and methods for tackling specific problems, through both written and oral formats.
Present scholarly arguments, logically and lucidly through both written and oral formats.

assessment_details.title: Thesis Draft
assessment_details.task_description: A complete draft of the thesis AND accompanying Turnitin report (PDF copy) must be presented for feedback from the Thesis Advisor(s). Please note that if you submit your draft after this date, you may not receive timely feedback. If your thesis is not available in draft form by this date, you should submit whatever written work you have as well as a Turnitin report. This is very important as it will give both you and your Thesis Advisor time to review your progress, address any issues, and plan the completion of your thesis. Failure to submit written drafts by this date will almost certainly mean a poor result for your final product. Please refer to the SOSC6014 Blackboard for detailed guidance on the suggested content and formatting of the thesis document. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submitted directly via email to the student's Thesis Advisor(s).
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
This is a formative assessment only. No extensions are required if you are unable to submit your thesis full draft and Turnitin report to your Thesis Advisor by the due date. You should notify your Thesis Advisor that the draft will be late and discuss a schedule for feedback and completion. Failure to submit your thesis draft by the due date will impact on receiving feedback from your Advisor.
Late submission
This is a formative assessment only. Failure to submit your thesis full draft and Turnitin report to your Thesis Advisor by the due date will not result in any penalty, but it will impact on receiving timely feedback from your Advisor.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessment_details.late_submission: This is a formative assessment only. Failure to submit your thesis full draft and Turnitin report to your Thesis Advisor by the due date will not result in any penalty, but it will impact on receiving timely feedback from your Advisor.

assessment_details.title: Thesis
assessment_details.task_description: Task: Students are to produce a thesis document between 12,000 and 15,000 words in length, which is the product of their own independent research undertaken during the Honours period. The thesis is to be written, structured, and formatted under the guidance of the student's Thesis Advisor(s) and in accordance with the guidelines provided on Blackboard. In addition to the written thesis, students are required to participate in a pass/fail oral defence hurdle about the thesis. Word count exclusions : text in figures, diagrams, and tables; fore-pages (title page up to the first page of the first or introductory chapter), appendices, and reference list or bibliography. Use of Artificial Intelligence Tools : This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst there may be instances where it is appropriate for students to use AI tools in their thesis project, successful completion of the thesis assessment will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. AI tools (including generative AI and machine translation tools) should be used with caution as they may lead to disputes about copyright, authorship, plagiarism, and research integrity. If you plan to use AI tools in the research or writing of your thesis, you MUST consult with your Thesis Advisor and/or Honours Convenor to discuss how you plan to use it, whether this usage is appropriate, how to document this usage, and how to acknowledge AI usage in your submitted assessment. A failure to reference AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Guidelines on how to reference AI in your assessment can be found here: https://guides.library.uq.edu.au/referencing/ai-tools-assignments (additional guidance will be provided on Blackboard). UQ guidelines on AI usage can be found at the UQ AI Student Hub : https://web.library.uq.edu.au/study-and-learning-support/ai-student-hub including UQ rules for using AI. Marking criteria and/or marking rubrics are available in the ‘Assessment’ folder in Blackboard for this course. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
You must submit your assessment electronically by the due time, on the due date.
Your assessment must be submitted via Turnitin on Blackboard Ultra. To submit your assessment electronically log in to Blackboard Ultra with your UQ username and password and navigate to the relevant submission link for that assessment item on Blackboard Ultra. No emailed submissions of assessments will be accepted.
Turnitin links will be configured to permit early submission of the thesis. Students will have the opportunity to submit draft thesis chapters to a dedicated Turnitin portal prior to submission of the final assignment in order to review similarity index content and to improve academic writing practice in accordance with UQ Academic Integrity policies.
By submitting your assessment through Turnitin, you confirm that all work is your own, except where properly referenced. Only submit assessments that contain your original work.
Please note that on the preview page, your thesis will be shown without formatting. Your thesis will retain formatting and your coordinator will be able to see formatted theses. Once you have submitted your thesis you are able to go back and view your submission with the correct formatting.
You are required to retain proof of submission of your assessment. Your Digital Receipt is available for download from your Assignment Dashboard. If you cannot see your submission and download your digital receipt within a few hours, your assessment has not been successfully submitted; please submit again.
If you are experiencing technical difficulties with Blackboard, please contact the ITS Support Team .
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
Discretionary extensions are not available for this task.
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Please note that from Semester 2, 2025 the Assessment Procedure has changed. You must submit your application on or before the assessment due date and time.
Grounds of an Extension
A list of acceptable reasons for an extension and the evidence you must provide can be found here . Your request may be refused if you do not meet the acceptable reasons for an extension. All extension requests must be accompanied by documentation that supports the reason for the request. The student submitting the request is fully responsible for all supporting documentation that is provided with the request and should ensure all documents are authentic.
Students registered with Student Support and Wellbeing Services / SAP
In the School of Social Science, extensions granted on the basis of registration with Student Support and Wellbeing Services (SSWS) or a Student Access Plan (SAP) will be the lesser of 7 calendar days or the maximum extension allowable for the assessment task.
More information is provided in Part D of the Assessment Procedure.
Discretionary Extensions
Discretionary extensions are NOT available for your Thesis.
Maximum Extension Requests
You can request a maximum of 3 extensions for the same assessment task. If a third extension is necessary, you must submit an Assessment Management Plan in addition to your supporting documentation with your request. If after the third approved extension you submit the assessment late, late penalties will apply.
Late Extension Applications
Late applications must include evidence of the reasons for the late request, detailing why you were unable to apply for an extension by the due date and time. The School of Social Science will not accept personal statements.
Additional information
Extension requests are processed and managed by the School of Social Science Administration Team. Course Coordinators are unable to grant extensions directly.
The maximum extension length is 14 calendar days.
Thesis extensions are highly likely to delay your graduation to the next graduation period.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
Assessments will not be accepted if it is more than one week (7 calendar days) late without prior approval.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Oral Defence
assessment_details.task_description: You are required to participate in an oral defence to assess whether you have attained the required learning outcomes of the thesis. Passing the oral defence is a hurdle, failure of the oral defence will result in a maximum grade of 3 for the thesis course, and you will be required to re-enrol in the course with a new thesis topic (subject to advisor availability). The oral defence will be marked on a pass/fail basis and be assessed against the following criteria: · Demonstrated ability to articulate the rationale underpinning the choice of central research question/s, the identification of a key gap in knowledge that the thesis addresses, and the devising of the central hypothesis/argument. · Demonstrated ability to justify the thesis structure. · Demonstrated ability to explain and justify the methodology/ies used. · Demonstrated mastery of knowledge, analysis, and findings presented in the thesis. · Ability to satisfactorily respond to spontaneous and pertinent questions in the oral defence. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Oral defence will be delivered in-person.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
Discretionary extensions are not available for this task.
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Please note, if you are approved an extension on your written thesis, your oral defence will also be delayed. The School will advise you as soon as possible of the revised date and time of the oral defence in the event of an approved extension of the written thesis. Also note that regardless of an extension timeframe/number of days for either the thesis or oral defence, this does not guarantee the oral defence will be conducted within that timeframe and it could take place later, as it will be dependent on availability of the attending academic staff members .
If you are unable to attend your oral defence due to sudden illness and/or exceptional circumstances, you are required to notify the Honours Convenor and your Supervisor as soon as possible and prior to the scheduled time of your defence .
Please note that from Semester 2, 2025 the Assessment Procedure has changed. A request for an extension to an assessment due date must be submitted through my.UQ as soon as it becomes evident that an extension is needed, but no later than one calendar day after the assessment task submission due date and time.
Grounds of an Extension
Extensions to the Oral Defence assessment will only be considered for exceptional circumstances. All extension requests must be accompanied by documentation that supports the reason for the request. The student submitting the request is fully responsible for all supporting documentation that is provided with the request and should ensure all documents are authentic.
Students registered with Student Support and Wellbeing Services / SAP
In the School of Social Science, extensions granted on the basis of registration with Student Support and Wellbeing Services (SSWS) or a Student Access Plan (SAP) will be the lesser of 7 calendar days or the maximum extension allowable for the assessment task.
More information is provided in Part D of the Assessment Procedure.
Discretionary Extensions
Discretionary extensions are NOT available for your Oral Defence.
Maximum Extension Requests
You can request a maximum of 3 extensions for the same assessment task. If a third extension is necessary, you must submit an Assessment Management Plan in addition to your supporting documentation with your request. If after the third approved extension you submit the assessment late, late penalties will apply.
Late Extension Applications
Late applications must include evidence of the reasons for the late request, detailing why you were unable to apply for an extension by the due date and time. The School of Social Science will not accept personal statements.
Additional information
Extension requests are processed and managed by the School of Social Science Administration Team. Course Coordinators are unable to grant extensions directly.
The maximum extension length is 14 calendar days.
Oral defence extensions are highly likely to delay your graduation to the next graduation period.
Late submission
You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late .
